settings.title: Five Years In, and Still Independent
settings.excerpt: We are marking our fifth year by sharing the one principle that has not changed since day one.
settings.body:
This month marks five years since Clearpath took on its first client, a two-person workshop that needed help pricing their work. They are still in business, and so are we.

A lot has changed in that time. We have worked with shops, studios, and service firms, and we have watched some grow well beyond what they imagined on the day they first called us. The one thing we have held onto is that we only take on work we believe we can move. Turning down a project we cannot help with has always served clients better than saying yes to everything.

The principle that has not moved

When we started, we made a quiet promise to ourselves that we would never bill for activity that did not change anything. It is an easy line to write and a hard one to keep, especially in a slow month. But every time we have honoured it, the client has stayed longer and trusted us more.

That single rule has shaped almost everything else about how we run. It is why our engagements stay short, why we are honest about what is outside our depth, and why we will sometimes point a caller toward someone better suited than us.

Looking back, a few things stand out about why we are still here and still independent.

  • We grew slowly enough to keep the work good
  • We turned down projects that were not a fit
  • We kept our advice plain and our invoices honest

Where the next five years go

We have no plan to become a bigger firm for the sake of it. The goal is the same as it was on day one, which is to be the people a small owner calls when they need a clear head and a straight answer.

To everyone who has trusted us with their business over five years, thank you. We are looking forward to the next stretch.
